{"step_by_step_thinking": "HELP syndrome stands for Hemolysis, Elevated Liver enzymes, and Low Platelets. Hemolysis refers to the breakdown of red blood cells, which leads to hemolytic anemia. Elevated liver enzymes and thrombocytopenia (low platelet count) are also key features of HELLP syndrome. Eosinophilia, which is an increase in eosinophils, is not a recognized feature of HELLP syndrome.", "answer_choice": "B"}
